source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/참조 문서/2. Software/2. Software Validation Report(Doc No. SVR-01, Rev.0) [62304 2014 적용].docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software Validation Report SVR-01 documenting software level of concern, IEC/EN 62304 Class B classification, requirements, design, verification, validation, traceability, anomalies, and release version.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation and regulatory evidence for medical device software, including FDA level of concern, IEC 62304 software safety classification, hazard analysis, verification and validation, and unresolved anomaly status.
keyPoints: The Level of Concern for the software is Moderate for FDA and Class B for IEC 62304.
The document states Document Number SVR-01 and revision history Rev. No. 0 Initial Release.
The preparation, review and approval dates are 2018.02.10.
The software release version number is Software version 1.0.
Unresolved Software Anomalies are listed as N/A.
Static testing, module review, integration review, dynamic testing, traceability analysis, and hazard analysis are included.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Software Validation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report

evidence.claim: The document number is SVR-01.
evidence.evidenceText: Document Number SVR-01

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev. 0 and the change description is Initial Release.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Initial Release

evidence.claim: The preparation, review and approval dates are 2018.02.10.
The report is according to EN 62304:2006/AC:2008.
evidence.evidenceText: According to EN 62304:2006/AC:2008

evidence.claim: The software level of concern is Moderate for FDA and Class B for IEC 62304.
evidence.evidenceText: Level of Concern: Moderate [FDA], Class B [IEC 62304]

evidence.claim: The software classification under EN 62304 is Class B because non-serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: B(Moderate) Non serious injury is possible O

evidence.claim: The risk management process references EN ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971 Standard specifies a process for monitoring administration of hazard.

evidence.claim: Risk factors are evaluated in accordance with EN ISO 14971:2012 Annex D.3, E.4 and H.2.3.5.
evidence.evidenceText: In accordance with the EN ISO 14971:2012 Annex D.3, E.4 and H.2.3.5, evaluate the risk factors for each risk

evidence.claim: The risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity x Probability

evidence.claim: Broadly Acceptable Zone is Risk Level 1 to 3, and As Far As Possible Zone is Risk Level 4 to 12.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Broadly Acceptable Zone Risk Level: 1~3 Red Zone As Far As Possible Zone Risk Level: 4~12

evidence.claim: The hardware platform is Atmega88 8bit Microprocessor.
evidence.evidenceText: Hardware Platform: Atmega88 8bit Microprocessor

evidence.claim: The main user interface device includes a power button used as an On/Off button.
evidence.evidenceText: Main Devices Power button: On/Off button

evidence.claim: Static testing and dynamic testing are the stated testing methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Testing Methods Static Testing & Dynamic Testing

evidence.claim: Static test items include code review, module review, and integration review performed by a software engineer.
evidence.evidenceText: Static Test a. Code Review: performed by S/W engineer. b. Module Review: performed by S/W engineer. c. Integration Review: performed by S/W engineer.

evidence.claim: Dynamic test is performed by Q.C and software engineer.
evidence.evidenceText: Dynamic Test a. Dynamic Test: performed by Q.C & S/W engineer.

evidence.claim: Verification and validation test results refer to appendices 1, 2, and 3.
evidence.evidenceText: V.V & Test Results Refer to appendix 1, 2, 3

evidence.claim: The software release version number is version 1.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Version Number - Software [version 1.0]

evidence.claim: Unresolved software anomalies are N/A.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Software Anomalies (Bugs or Defects) N/A

evidence.claim: The revision level history lists release date 2017.12.05 and release version 1.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Level History Release Date Release Version Before Change After Change Potential Effect 2017.12.05 1.0 None None None

evidence.claim: The safety module stops device operation after 30 minutes of use.
evidence.evidenceText: The safety module is responsible for stopping the device. It stop the operation of the MINUET LCD & MINUET LCD eco after 30minutes of use.

evidence.claim: The error module resets the program by activating the watch dog when an unexpected error occurs.
evidence.evidenceText: If unexpected error is occurred, the device will set the watch dog flag it will reset the program.

evidence.claim: The dynamic test report includes tests for device start, operate functions, LCD functions, error handling, and time display.
evidence.evidenceText: Dynamic Test Report Test case Item Method Criteria Count Remarks DT1 Start the device DT2 Operate DT3 LCD

reviewFlags.reason: Product Name and Model Name fields are present but blank in the document header.
Product evidence is conflicting or mixed: the source metadata/path signals HAD1417MCW or HAD/A/F 1417-1717, while the document text names MINUET LCD, MINUET LCD eco, and also references XVISION-525.
Authority is inferred from the metadata authoritySignal FDA and the text line 'Level of Concern: Moderate [FDA]'; no FDA-issued document number or FDA approval status is stated.
Several document fields and tables appear incomplete or templated, including Product Name, Model Name, role names, software tools, and many SRS function descriptions.
Hazard Analysis Result section says detailed analysis report refers to Appendix 3, but the document contents list Appendix 3 as Dynamic Test Report and Appendix 4 as Hazard Analysis Report.
Hazard HA-03 risk re-evaluation shows risk 2 but result 'Unacceptable', while similar risk 2 entries are marked Acceptable.
Document date is taken from preparation/review/approval dates; the document does not explicitly label a separate effective date.
Source text contains OCR or extraction quality issues such as misspellings, malformed headings, and inconsistent appendix numbering.
